---
title: "Hobo logger temperature data"
author: "Jill Ashey"
date: "2026-05-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(tidyverse)
```

Read in Hobo files  
```{r, include=F}
all_files <- list.files(path = "../data/environmental/hobo_loggers/", pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
data_files <- all_files[str_detect(basename(all_files), "^\\d+\\.csv$")]

# function to read in hobo data 
read_hobo_file <- function(file_path) {
  read_csv(file_path, skip = 1, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    # Select only the 2nd and 3rd columns by their index position
    select(2, 3) %>% 
    # Give them consistent names so they can be joined seamlessly
    set_names(c("DateTime", "Temp"))}

long_data <- data_files %>%
  set_names(basename(.)) %>% 
  map_df(read_hobo_file, .id = "SN") %>%
  mutate(SN = str_remove(SN, "\\.csv$")) 
```

Read in metadata and merge by serial number
```{r}
logger_metadata <- read_csv("../data/environmental/hobo_loggers/logger_experiment_metadata.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(Serial_number = as.character(Serial_number))

merged_data <- long_data %>%
  left_join(logger_metadata, by = c("SN" = "Serial_number")) %>%
  mutate(DateTime = mdy_hms(DateTime))
```

## Treatment tanks 

Select treatment tanks only 
```{r}
trt_data <- merged_data %>%
  filter(Tank %in% c("A1", "A2", "A3", "H1", "H2", "H3"))
```

Plot temperature by incubator colored by treatment. 

```{r}
trt_data<-trt_data%>%
  filter(DateTime >= ymd_hms("2026-05-03 09:00:00") & 
         DateTime <= ymd_hms("2026-05-24 08:00:00")) 

ggplot(trt_data, aes(x=DateTime, y=Temp, colour=Treatment))+
  geom_point()+
  #ylim(26, 30)+
  scale_colour_manual(values=c("blue", "red"), name="Temperature")+
  ylab("Temperature (°C)")+
  xlab("Date Time")+
  theme_classic()
```

Group by treatment and plot
```{r}
plot_summary <- trt_data %>%
  # Round to the nearest hour (change "1 hour" to "30 mins" or "15 mins" if preferred)
  mutate(Time_Interval = floor_date(DateTime, unit = "15 mins")) %>%
  group_by(Treatment, Time_Interval) %>%
  summarize(
    Mean_Temp = mean(Temp, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Temp   = sd(Temp, na.rm = TRUE),
    N         = n(),
    SE_Temp   = SD_Temp / sqrt(N),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

# 2. Plot the Mean Line and SE Shaded Ribbon
ggplot(plot_summary, aes(x = Time_Interval, y = Mean_Temp, color = Treatment, fill = Treatment)) +
  # Shaded region for SE (alpha controls transparency)
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Mean_Temp - SD_Temp, ymax = Mean_Temp + SD_Temp), 
              color = NA, alpha = 0.2) +
  # Solid mean line
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  # Custom aesthetic refinements
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Ambient" = "blue", "High" = "red")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Ambient" = "blue", "High" = "red")) +
  scale_x_datetime(date_labels = "%b %d", date_breaks = "1 week") +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Date Time",
    y = "Temperature (°C)",
    title = "Experimental Treatment Profiles (Mean ± SE)",
    color = "Treatment",
    fill = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

ggplot(plot_summary, aes(x = Time_Interval, y = Mean_Temp, color = Treatment, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Mean_Temp - SE_Temp, ymax = Mean_Temp + SE_Temp), 
              color = NA, 
              alpha = 0.4) + # Increased from 0.2 to 0.4 to make it darker
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) + # Slightly thinned the main line so the ribbon can peek out
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Ambient" = "blue", "High" = "red")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Ambient" = "blue", "High" = "red")) +
  scale_x_datetime(date_labels = "%b %d", date_breaks = "1 week") +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Date Time",
    y = "Temperature (°C)",
    title = "Experimental Treatment Profiles (Mean ± SE)"
  ) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 14))
```
